task_completion.description: Load the CT Cranium DICOM series into InVesalius 3
task_completion.visual_evidence: The InVesalius 3 interface is open, displaying axial, sagittal, and coronal views of a human skull/cranium CT scan.

A colored mask overlay (usually green or another distinct color) is visible on the 2D CT slices, specifically highlighting the high-density bone structures. The threshold panel may show a minimum value around 226 HU.
The 3D viewport in InVesalius displays a reconstructed 3D surface model of the skull.

task_completion.description: Record at least five linear cranial measurements
task_completion.visual_evidence: At least five distinct linear measurement annotations (lines with distance values in mm) are visible on the 2D slices or 3D view. The measurements should span significant anatomical distances (e.g., transverse, anteroposterior, skull height).

task_completion.description: Export the STL file and save the InVesalius project
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal, or InVesalius save dialogs showing 'implant_sizing.stl' and 'implant_plan.inv3' being saved to the /home/ga/Documents/ directory.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the tasks
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent clicking through the InVesalius interface, using the segmentation, 3D surface, and measurement tools, rather than just running a pre-written script in the terminal.

integrity.description: Measurements are genuinely drawn on the anatomical features
integrity.visual_evidence: The measurement lines visually correspond to actual skull dimensions (e.g., spanning across the cranium) and are not just randomly placed short lines to bypass the count requirement.

integrity.description: The 3D model matches the loaded CT scan
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D surface mesh in the viewer clearly resembles the segmented skull from the 2D slices, confirming it was generated from the DICOM data and not imported from an external source.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The agent must record at least 5 linear measurements, and valid measurements are expected to be at least 50.0 mm in length. The bone segmentation threshold should use a minimum of 226 HU. The final outputs must be exactly named 'implant_sizing.stl' (which should be a valid mesh with >10,000 triangles) and 'implant_plan.inv3', both located in /home/ga/Documents/.
